title: 2026 Ireland Graduate Civil Engineering Career Path: Water Sector
description: **We will close for applications on Wednesday 22nd April 2026, however, if this opportunity becomes over\-subscribed, we will close early, therefore we recommend applying as soon as possible.** Before making an application, please browse all graduate opportunities available as we will only accept **one application** per candidate. Once you have made an application you will not be able to make changes to your selection or make a new application. The exception is if we open new vacancies in the new year. **To avoid missing out on your preferred role, we recommend applying as early as possible, as positions will close once filled.** Turn your ambition into action \- apply now and shape your future with us. **Job Description** Designing Resilient Infrastructure for a Sustainable Future. Join a team that’s shaping tomorrow’s water systems—today. AtMott MacDonald, our Water Division is a centre of excellence, tackling some of the most pressing challenges of our time: climate change, ageing infrastructure, and population growth. As aGraduate Civil Engineer, you’ll play a vital role in designing safe, sustainable water and wastewater systems that serve communities now and for generations to come. This is more than a graduate role—it’s an opportunity to grow, collaborate, and make a meaningful impact. You’ll be supported by a caring and inclusive team, empowered to innovate, and inspired to contribute to projects that improve lives and protect the environment. What You’ll Be Doing You’ll work alongside multidisciplinary teams of engineers, scientists, and consultants, contributing to projects that blend technical excellence with social purpose. **Your Responsibilities May Include** * Designing Infrastructure \- Contribute to the design of pipelines, water and wastewater treatment facilities across feasibility, outline, and detailed design stages. * Collaborative Delivery \- Work with diverse teams to develop integrated, sustainable solutions that reflect the needs of communities and ecosystems. * Project Lifecycle Support \- Be involved from planning and modelling through to construction, commissioning, and handover—gaining holistic project experience. * Digital Innovation \- Use cutting\-edge digital tools to enhance design quality, efficiency, and collaboration. Why This Role Matters * Empowering Growth \- You’ll receive mentoring, structured training, and hands\-on experience to build your technical and project management skills. * Celebrating Impact \- Every project you contribute to helps deliver clean water, protect natural resources, and build resilience against climate change. * Inclusive Culture \- We celebrate diversity and foster a culture where everyone feels valued, heard, and supported. * Integrity \& Trust \- We uphold the highest standards of professionalism and ethics, building trust with clients, communities, and each other. * Wellbeing First \- Your wellbeing is our priority. We offer flexible working, mental health support, and a caring environment where you can thrive. * Purpose\-Driven Work \- Your engineering solutions will have real\-world impact—enhancing public health, environmental sustainability, and community resilience. **Candidate Specification** We are looking for committed and motivated graduates with a genuine passion and a desire to make a difference in the world. If this describes you, apply today to launch your career at Mott MacDonald. To be eligible for this opportunity, you will have less than 12 months’ relevant experience **(excluding placements)** and have a bachelor's degree (obtained no earlier than 2024\) or expected to achieve a degree in **civil engineering** or similar disciplines: In your application, you should aim to demonstrate your genuine interest in both the opportunity and in joining Mott MacDonald. You can do this by highlighting any relevant experience or interests \- such as modules you've studied, previous work experience, or any research you've undertaken about our company, values, and projects. We personally review every completed application form we receive as this part of our selection process is entirely human led – we do not use AI tools at this stage and kindly expect you not to either. We want you to answer each application question with authenticity so we can get to know the real you. Joining us as a graduate is your opportunity to shape your own story. **We Are Looking For Graduates With The Following Strengths** * Proactively takes initiative to complete tasks efficiently and independently. * Communicates effectively and clearly with clients and colleagues. * Delivers high quality written work with strong attention to detail and clarity. * Demonstrates a structured and analytical approach to problem solving. **About Your Development** A graduate position should be more than just a job. We know this and so do you. That’s why with our graduate roles we aim to give you the experience and technical knowledge you need to progress your career. From the moment you join us, you’ll receive the training you need. You will be assigned a mentor, who will guide you to meet the objectives of your professional training needs. You will join our three\-year early careers soft skills development programme – Accelerating your Future, which is designed to give you the skills and tools necessary for a long\-lasting successful career here at Mott MacDonald. You will have the opportunity to make a difference; learn more about Our Purpose and the difference we can make! You’re probably wondering what else is on offer.Join us, and you’ll get: * Biannual salary reviews: we believe that hard work should be rewarded and recognised. Therefore, for the first three years of your career with us, you’ll have biannual salary reviews. * A competitive salary: in addition to biannual reviews, we will ensure that you’re given a salary that matches the current industry standard. **Our social side** Being part of Mott MacDonald means more than just work; there’s a huge range of fun and exciting things that you can get involved in. From the moment you join us, you’ll have the opportunity to expand your social and professional network, whether it’s meeting other graduates or joining forces with other members of staff from around the company at our annual sports day. Each of our offices have a sports and social committee which will give you the chance to be part of a variety of sports, social and charity events. We’re committed to promoting a strong culture of social responsibility and encourage our staff to play active roles in the local community. It's worth noting that sports and social committees tend to vary from office to office, so as well as getting involved in what’s already on offer, don’t be afraid to suggest new events or initiatives that you think could be a great addition. It doesn’t stop there. As well as events, you’ll also have access to discounts on cinema tickets, travel, fashion, utilities and much more as part of our company benefits which you can take advantage of the moment you join. **Equality, diversity and inclusion** We put equality, diversity and inclusion at the heart of our business, seeking to promote fair employment procedures and practices to ensure equal opportunities for all. We encourage individual expression in our workplace and are committed to creating an inclusive environment where everyone feels they have the opportunity to contribute. **Agile working** At Mott MacDonald, we believe it makes business sense for you and your manager to choose how you can work most effectively to meet your client, team and personal commitments. We embrace agility, flexibility and trust. **More About Mott MacDonald** We’re a global engineering, management and development consultancy. Our purpose is to improve society by considering social outcomes in everything we do, relentlessly focusing on excellence and digital innovation, transforming our clients’ businesses, our communities and employee opportunities. A fundamental part of this is respecting each person’s differences and striving to meet their needs. Our values: progress, respect, integrity, drive, excellence Location(s): Dublin, IE **Contract Type:** Permanent **Work Pattern:** Full Time Market: Water **Discipline:** Civil **Job Ref:** 11504 **Recruiter Contact:** earlycareers.recruitment@mottmac.com

title: AWS DevOps Engineer – Dublin/Remote (2 days onsite pw)
description: **AWS DevOps Engineer – Dublin/Remote (2 days onsite pw)** My client, based in Dublin City, is a leader in providing technology to Life Science regulatory processes and is continuing to expand its Platform team. They have partnered with IT Search to engage a mid\-level AWS DevOps Engineer to work alongside Engineering Heads and work on complex issues relating to automation, CI/CD, business logic, security and design matters. ***The role:*** * Develop strong internal relationships with Developers and Quality teams to deliver architecture on an ongoing basis, working closely with Architects to execute pipelines * Focus on areas such as security at scale, taking ownership of high quality output across deployment and create/manage/amend CI/CD pipelines to a deep level * Automation through (Python or similar) * There will be some involvement in incident response when required * Evidence based attitude to work, ability to back up ideas and recognise errors and change course accordingly * Proven experience of AWS Services, Pipelining, Kubernetes, Terraform and Python ***Role Requirements:*** * Approximately 5 years of commercial experience in a Platform/DevOps Environment * Ideally have worked in depth with Kubernetes Clusters (maintaining as well as building rather than simply deploying), AWS Services (as a preference), IaC (Terraform ideally), Python/Go on a daily basis for ongoing scripting and automation This is a high potential position with a well\-established firm to join a highly experienced and talented team, with a strong benefits package and work life balance. To learn more, please share your latest CV to arrange a confidential conversation, however, please note we can only accept applications from candidates eligible to work on a permanent basis in Ireland.

title: Accountant
description: **New Opportunity \| Recently Qualified Accountant \| Newbridge** Pro Recruitment Ireland is delighted to be partnering with a growing accountancy practice in Newbridge to hire a recently qualified accountant. This is a fantastic opportunity to step into a role where you’ll own your own client portfolio, work closely with the Practice Owner, and play a key role in the firm’s continued growth. **The Role:** Manage a diverse portfolio of clients. Prepare and review financial accounts and tax computations. Support management accounts and audit processes. Build strong client relationships and contribute to business growth. Mentor junior team members. **What We’re Looking For:** ACA / ACCA / CPA qualified. 2–3 years’ experience in an Irish practice. Strong technical and client\-facing skills. Organised, proactive, and commercially aware. **What’s on Offer:** €50,000 – €60,000 DOE. Hybrid working (1–2 days/week). 25 days annual leave. Strong work\-life balance. Ongoing training and career progression. Location: Newbridge, Co. Kildare If you’re looking to take the next step in your career within a supportive and ambitious firm, we’d love to hear from you. Apply now or message me directly for a confidential chat.

title: Agile Quality Coach - Day Rate Contract, Dublin
description: **Agile Quality Coach \- Day Rate Contract, Dublin/Hybrid** Talentspot is exclusively supporting a central\-Dublin based customer in their search for an Agile Quality/Test Coach. This is a day\-rate contract with hybrid (2\-3 days a week) remote\-working. The emphasis of this new position is hands\-on testing and bug\-bashing, with a typical split of 60/40 vs coaching developers in your scrum team and driving all things quality. Are you enthusiastic about technology and passionate about Agile software testing? Can you communicate in technical and non\-technical terms which parts of the software are covered and where the business risks are from a testing perspective? As a member of a Dublin\-based Quality coaching team, you will join them in having defect prevention at the centre of what you do, achieving this by collaborating with team members to build quality into engineering processes. You’ll bring the ability to understand a product from the end user’s perspective and test that product with usability in mind. You’ll work in an evolved agile environment and deep experience in this space is essential as they utilise the best of agile methodologies to advocate for quality. You’ll possess a quality\-driven, collaborative approach as a default, maintaining a clear focus on continuous improvement. **Role \& Responsibilities** * Work as an Agile Quality/Test Coach in a fast\-paced environment to help maintain and improve product quality * Serve as the sole QA representative in an agile scrum team consisting of 4\+ developers * Train and coach developers and team members on risk\-based techniques, mindmaps, and exploratory test techniques * Collaborative in your approach to testing, including performing in dev/pair testing with developers * Act as a change agent by leading and being the point of contact for all Quality / Test process improvements for your team * Continuously monitor team quality processes and production quality, and report on team metrics * Encourage colleagues to drive continuous improvements * Work with UI automation engineers to continuously review and improve the automated regression suite, as well as lead the release plan for automation * Ensure teams adhere to agreed test automation guidelines and standards * Facilitate mob test sessions with your team to further test high\-risk features and show the teams the value in exploratory testing * Participate in early quality solutioning workshops when required * Take a collaborative approach to story refining and use story points as a metric for estimation * Identify quality issues, defects, performance issues and collaborate across teams to achieve issue resolution **Technical Skills \& Experience** * Strong knowledge and proven experience in software QA methodologies, tools and processes * 5\+ years in software quality assurance, test design and execution * 2\-3\+ years’ experience working in an Agile environment using Scrum * Good understanding of test automation guidelines and experience in defining automated test coverage for a product or feature * Ability to test mobile apps across different OS * SQL knowledge * Ownership of testing tools * Commercial experience with Agentic AI, Chrome plug\-ins, and Copilot Studio * Maintain an up\-to\-date knowledge of the process and industry developments **Soft Skills** * An analytical approach to problem finding – bring a proactive approach, spotting recurring errors in production, quality themes and patterns within the team, and create solutions to these issues * Self\-starter and able to motivate those around you * Bring a collaborative approach to testing * Facilitate mob test sessions with your scrum team to further test high\-risk features and to demonstrate the value of exploratory testing * Experience in leading Quality efforts in an Agile team is an advantage * Encourage colleagues to drive continuous improvements * Act as a change agent by leading and being the point of contact for all quality/testing process improvements for your team * Bring a collaborative approach to story refining and use story points as an estimation metric * Excellent communicator and facilitator of team sessions * Drive continuous improvement in teams You must be eligible to live in Ireland working as a day\-rate contractor (possess an EU/UK passport or Stamp 4 visa).

title: Android Developer - Associate
description: **JOB DESCRIPTION** Join our mobile engineering team and help shape the future of Android development at JPMorganChase. You will create impactful, modular applications and frameworks that drive our digital strategy. As a Software Engineer III, you’ll expand your skills, collaborate with talented colleagues, and make a difference every day. Experience a supportive culture where your ideas are valued and your expertise drives innovation. Discover opportunities for growth and advancement in a dynamic environment. As a Software Engineer III in our Mobile Engineering team, you will specialize in Android application and SDK development, building native Android experiences using Kotlin and Java. You will work with us to design modular, reusable Android components and applications that enhance our mobile platforms. You will collaborate with product managers and engineers, participate in architectural decisions, and utilize AI\-assisted development tools. Your contributions will help us deliver secure, scalable, and user\-friendly mobile solutions. **Job responsibilities** * Develop Android applications using Kotlin and Java. * Build and maintain Android SDK and framework components. * Implement Android APIs and platform features. * Write comprehensive unit and instrumentation tests. * Design modular, reusable Android components. * Collaborate with mobile team and product managers. * Participate in architectural decisions for Android applications. * Utilize AI\-assisted development tools. **Required qualifications, capabilities, and skills** * Minimum 4 years of Android development experience. * Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science or related field, or equivalent experience. * Strong foundation in Android development patterns and architecture. * Experience with Android SDK and framework development. * Proficiency in Kotlin and Java programming. * Expertise in Android SDK/framework development and Android APIs. * Experience using GitHub Copilot. **Preferred qualifications, capabilities, and skills** * Experience designing modular, reusable Android components. * Familiarity with AI\-assisted development tools. * Strong collaboration skills with cross\-functional teams. * Ability to participate in architectural decisions and drive innovation. **ABOUT US** J.P. Morgan is a global leader in financial services, providing strategic advice and products to the world’s most prominent corporations, governments, wealthy individuals and institutional investors. Our first\-class business in a first\-class way approach to serving clients drives everything we do. We strive to build trusted, long\-term partnerships to help our clients achieve their business objectives. We recognize that our people are our strength and the diverse talents they bring to our global workforce are directly linked to our success. We are an equal opportunity employer and place a high value on diversity and inclusion at our company. We do not discriminate on the basis of any protected attribute, including race, religion, color, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, age, marital or veteran status, pregnancy or disability, or any other basis protected under applicable law. We also make reasonable accommodations for applicants’ and employees’ religious practices and beliefs, as well as mental health or physical disability needs. Visit our FAQs for more information about requesting an accommodation. **ABOUT THE TEAM** Our Corporate Technology team relies on smart, driven people like you to develop applications and provide tech support for all our corporate functions across our network. Your efforts will touch lives all over the financial spectrum and across all our divisions: Global Finance, Corporate Treasury, Risk Management, Human Resources, Compliance, Legal, and within the Corporate Administrative Office. You’ll be part of a team specifically built to meet and exceed our evolving technology needs, as well as our technology controls agenda.

title: Architect Conservation Historical BIM Revit
description: **Job Title** Conservation Architect **Grade / Level** Intermediate Senior Professional / Specialist Grade **Reporting To** Principal Architect / Head of Conservation **Location** National — with travel to monument sites as required **Contract Type** Permanent / Fixed\-Term (Public Sector) **Salary** Commensurate with public sector pay scales (RIAI Grade) **Role Overview** We are seeking an experienced and dedicated Conservation Architect to join our team in delivering high\-quality conservation and restoration works on Ireland's most significant protected national monuments. Working directly for a public service client, the successful candidate will lead and contribute to complex heritage projects that safeguard the built environment for future generations. This is a rewarding opportunity for a passionate conservation professional to apply specialist skills within a multi\-disciplinary team, working across an exceptional portfolio of State\-owned historical structures, protected buildings, and scheduled monuments. **Key Responsibilities** **Project Delivery** * Lead and manage conservation and restoration projects on national monuments from inception through to completion, ensuring compliance with conservation best practice and statutory obligations. * Prepare, coordinate, and review architectural drawings, specifications, schedules of works, and contract documentation for conservation projects. * Undertake detailed condition surveys, building pathology investigations, and heritage impact assessments on protected structures. * Develop and implement Conservation Management Plans (CMPs) and Conservation Plans in accordance with ICOMOS standards and national policy. * Manage contractor and specialist sub\-contractor performance on site, ensuring quality, programme, and cost targets are met. **Technical \& BIM** * Produce and coordinate Building Information Models (BIM) using Autodesk Revit, developing Heritage BIM (HBIM) workflows appropriate to existing historic fabric. * Ensure BIM deliverables meet client EIR (Employer's Information Requirements) and comply with ISO 19650 standards. * Utilise point cloud and photogrammetric survey data to inform accurate as\-existing models of historic structures. * Coordinate BIM inputs across multi\-disciplinary teams including structural, mechanical, electrical, and conservation specialists. **Compliance \& Statutory** * Liaise with the Department of Housing, Local Government and Heritage, the National Monuments Service, and other statutory authorities on consents, permissions, and compliance. * Prepare and manage applications for Ministerial Consent under the National Monuments Acts and relevant planning permissions. * Ensure all works comply with the Building Regulations, Conservation Principles (OPW/DHLGH), and relevant EU Directives. * Maintain up\-to\-date knowledge of Irish and European heritage legislation, policy, and best conservation practice. **Stakeholder \& Client Management** * Act as a primary point of contact for the public sector client, providing regular project updates and technical advice. * Engage proactively with heritage stakeholders, community groups, and government departments as required. * Contribute to public outreach, interpretation, and educational activities associated with national monuments. * Mentor and support junior architectural and technical staff within the team. **Essential Requirements** **Qualification \& Registration** **Experience** ✔ Professional degree in Architecture (RIBA/RIAI Part III equivalent or higher) ✔ Full registration with the Royal Institute of the Architects of Ireland (RIAI) ✔ Post\-graduate qualification in Architectural Conservation (desirable) ✔ Minimum 5 years' post\-qualification experience in architectural conservation ✔ Demonstrable experience working on protected structures and/or national monuments ✔ Experience with public sector procurement and contracts (GCCC / RIAI forms) **Desirable Skills \& Advantageous Experience** * Proficiency in Autodesk Revit and BIM authoring tools, with experience in Heritage BIM (HBIM) methodologies — experience with BIM is considered a significant advantage for this role. * Familiarity with point cloud software (e.g. Leica Cyclone, Autodesk ReCap) and photogrammetric workflows for existing structure capture. * Knowledge of traditional building materials and crafts, including lime mortars, natural stone, historic masonry, and timber. * Experience with the Office of Public Works (OPW) or other state heritage bodies. * Demonstrated ability to prepare and present successful Ministerial Consent applications. * Competence in AutoCAD and/or ArchiCAD as supplementary tools. * Strong written communication skills with experience preparing Heritage Impact Assessments, Conservation Reports, and Condition Surveys. **Personal Competencies** * Commitment to the principles and ethics of conservation architecture and sustainable heritage management. * Strong organisational and project management capability, able to manage multiple concurrent projects. * Excellent interpersonal and communication skills, including the ability to engage diverse stakeholders. * High attention to detail and a methodical approach to technical documentation. * Collaborative team player with the ability to lead and mentor others. * A genuine passion for Ireland's built heritage and the public interest in its protection. **What We Offer** * The opportunity to work on some of Ireland's most iconic and historically significant national monuments. * A supportive public service environment with a strong emphasis on professional development and CPD. * Access to cutting\-edge heritage survey technology and specialist conservation resources. * Competitive public sector salary and benefits. * Flexible and hybrid working arrangements where operationally feasible. * Meaningful, purpose\-driven work that contributes directly to the preservation of Ireland's national identity. **How to Apply** Interested candidates should submit a current curriculum vitae, a covering letter addressing the essential and desirable criteria, and a portfolio of relevant conservation projects (max. 10 pages) to the address below. **Applications are assessed on a rolling basis. Early application is strongly encouraged.** *This public sector body is an equal opportunities employer and welcomes applications from all suitably qualified persons.*

title: Architectural Graduate
description: Location: Dublin Employer's Address 1 Grantham Street, Dublin, D08 A49Y Closing Date: 07 May 2026 Employer's Website https://douglaswallace.com/ Employer's Email info@douglaswallace.com Job Descrption Douglas Wallace Consultants have an opportunity for an Architectural Graduate to join our office in Dublin 8\. We are seeking applications from candidates with 2\+ years of experience to work on a broad range of projects across the country at varying scales and stages of development. Job Requirements * Relevant qualification in Architecture * Candidates should have a minimum of 2\+ years of experience in practice. * Experience working with both CAD and REVIT is essential. * Experience of multi\-unit residential projects is preferred. * Working knowledge of Irish building regulations and statutory processes. * High standard of spoken and written English. * Full eligibility to work in Ireland

title: Architectural Technologist
description: Location: Dublin/Monaghan Employer's Address 64 Mount Street Lower, Dublin 2, D02 TH77, Ireland / Unit 3, Lakeside Walk, North Road, Monaghan H18 AW26 Closing Date: 09 Apr 2026 Employer's Website https://hyphen.archi/ Employer's Email dbeggan@hyphen.archi Job Description As an architectural technologist at Hyphen, you’ll typically be engaged on a major client account, working with a range of stakeholders (both in and outside the business), and a project team. Most of our projects are in the commercial sectors of data centres, logistics, offices, retail and hospitality. A large proportion of our work is based outside Ireland so travel opportunities are available for those wishing to experience working on projects somewhere new. This role would suit a recently qualified architectural technologist but applications are also welcomed from other relevant professional backgrounds. We encourage all our employees to work at the highest standards of professionalism whilst demonstrating initiative and independence of thought. In return, we’ll provide you with the experience and education to progress your career: * Most of our projects pass rapidly from concept to completion so you’ll experience a wide range of job phases, including valuable site experience * Each of our local offices are relatively small so you’ll quickly feel part of a friendly, supportive team, and get to work closely with senior colleagues * Our international network means you’ll be given the opportunity to collaborate with Hyphen’s offices across Europe and Latin America and work on interesting projects for world\-leading clients In addition, our hybrid working approach means that you’ll be able to work from home for up to three days each week with the remaining time in our Monaghan or Dublin Office. If you feel that Hyphen is a place where you can contribute your best work, be proud of your achievements and see your career develop we’d love to hear from you. Job Requirements * A minimum of two years’ post\-graduate experience, preferably across all project workstages in a relevant sector * A good understanding of Irish, EU, UK or US permitting and construction * Strong technical design skills and knowledge * Solid working knowledge of Revit, AutoCAD and MS Office * Confident verbal and written communication skills * Excellent team working skills and a positive, pro\-active attitude * Great organisational skills, with the ability to prioritise tasks and meet deadlines * Fluent in written and spoken English (proficiency in one or more additional languages would also be advantageous)

title: Assoc. Spclst, Quality Control
description: Job Description A fantastic opportunity has arisen for an **Associate Technical Specialist Biochemistry \& Cell Biology** in our Dunboyne facility. This individual will be responsible for quality control support testing by providing technical expertise for the following activities: method execution troubleshooting, establishment of methods, input in investigations, building capability related to Biochemistry and Cell Biology methods and Analytical methods. Our facility in Dunboyne joined our network in September 2020\. Our Dunboyne site is our Company’s first Biologics Drug Substance Single Use Commercialisation Facility, playing an important role in helping to commercialise key medicines. The cutting\-edge innovation and collaboration between our Company’s research and manufacturing teams will significantly accelerate the time it takes to bring a medicine to market for the benefit of patients around the world. **Bring energy, knowledge, innovation to carry out the following:** * Develop lead and coach the Quality Control team in relation to the technical aspect of the lab activities and drive a high\-performance organisational culture that accommodates rapid growth and highly dynamic expectations, ensuring attainment of site operational and development goals. * Support the performance and optimisation of advanced assays requiring precise analytical skills and an understanding of biological and chemical principles. * Perform advanced assays requiring precise analytical skills and an understanding of biological and chemical principles. * Complete special project/protocol testing in a timely manner under direction of Project Leads and/or Area Lead. * Works under minimal supervision. * Prepare and update SOPs as required. * Drive the development of technical skills through coaching and motivate a high performing culture across the team. * Provide support and expertise for the preparation of regulatory submissions, inspection readiness, health authority inspections including interaction with inspectors, responses to inspection observations, and title: Associate
description: **🌍Job Title: Associate/Principal Engineer – Water** **Division: Water Engineering** **Location: Dublin/Cork, Ireland** **💡About Us** Ayesa Engineering is a global engineering consultancy with over 13,000 employees, where our UK \& Ireland business combines international strength with the agility and autonomy of an SME. With a presence in 40 countries, Ayesa Engineering has built a strong international reputation across a diverse range of engineering disciplines. Our expertise includes water and flood infrastructure, transportation, marine and coastal engineering, geoscience, environmental services, energy, and building structures. Our UK \& Ireland teams deliver technically challenging, varied projects that develop broad expertise without confining people to narrow roles — offering the opportunity to make a meaningful local impact while benefiting from the stability, knowledge, and career opportunities of a well\-established global organisation. **What You Will Be Doing:** As an Associate/Principal Engineer, you’ll lead and deliver Wastewater and Water design packages across diverse sectors including commercial, residential, education, healthcare, and public infrastructure. We require a self\-driving engineer at senior level to manage predominantly UÉ related work but also to support on our civil designs for contractors. **Key Responsibilities** * As the technical lead, you will ensure all designs meet the specific Standard Details and Codes of Practice set by Uisce Éireann or other relevant authority. * Design Lead: Spearhead the design of water distribution networks, foul sewerage collection systems, pumping stations and general civil engineering. * Hydraulic Modelling: Oversee or perform complex hydraulic analysis for water and wastewater networks using software packages. * Flood Risk: Prepare and review Site\-Specific Flood Risk Assessments in accordance with "The Planning System and Flood Risk Management" guidelines. * Understanding the Irish planning landscape is critical for moving a project from concept to construction. Understanding of the Irish property acquisition and wayleave process. * Planning Submissions: Prepare technical reports and drawings for Part 8 (Local Authority) or Strategic Infrastructure Development (SID) planning applications. * Statutory Liaising: Negotiate with Local Authorities, the Environmental Protection Agency (EPA), and Uisce Éireann for connections and/or design diversions. * Environmental Coordination: Collaborate with ecologists / hydrologists / archaeologists to ensure designs comply with Appropriate Assessments (AA) and Environmental Impact Assessment Reports (EIAR). * Contract Documentation: Draft tender documents using the Capital Works Management Framework (CWMF) for public projects or FIDIC/NEC4 for private/utility contracts. * Contract Administration: Act as the "Employer’s Representative" managing claims, variations, and payment certificates. * Procurement: Evaluate tenders and provide technical recommendations to clients for contractor selection. * You will be responsible to work in multidisciplinary teams while ensuring legal safety compliance. * PSDP Role: Fulfil the duties of the Project Supervisor for the Design Process (PSDP) under the 2006 Safety, Health and Welfare at Work (Construction) Regulations. * Financial Oversight: Monitor project budgets, resource allocation (junior engineers / technicians), and ensure the "man\-hour" targets are met. * Quality Assurance: Implement and maintain ISO quality standards for all deliverables, ensuring "Right First Time" submissions to clients. * Site Inspections: Conduct surveys and oversee site investigation (SI) works to inform the design phase. * Snagging \& Handover: Manage the final commissioning of water infrastructure, ensuring all "As\-Built" drawings and O\&M manuals are accepted by the end\-user. * Management of relationships with key clients and contacts across the business including client liaison and development. * Management of projects within time, cost and quality constraints and standards * Management of the day\-to\-day work of the team. * Champions and promotes professional advice, guidance and support across the business. * Monitors and manages compliance and KPI’s * Estimating, pricing and delivering against work targets. * Management of engineering outsource partners. **What You Bring:** * Exposure to some or all of the following: drinking water treatment and distribution, wastewater collection and treatment, drainage and flood risk management and assessment. * General Civil Engineering Design/Water infrastructure design experience. * Ability to plan, organise and manage resources, provide leadership, mentor and motivate a team of Engineers. * Can effectively communicate with clients, colleagues, external agencies and build effective working relationships. * Commercially astute, prepare technical and commercial bids, win work, project manage and consistently deliver significant projects to program and budget. * Take ownership of multiple projects managing risk, ensuring financial, commercial and contractual compliance. * Able to promote and champion a quality culture to achieve company goals, is committed to Continued Professional Development and keeps up to date with current trends in the industry. * Proven experience in Project Management of large Water Infrastructure Projects. * Experience of contract administration, procurement procedures and contract forms an advantage. * Experience as an External Service Provider (ESP) with Uisce Éireann will be a distinct advantage. * Degree qualified in relevant field * Relevant professional qualification: Engineering/Project Management * Minimum of 10 to 15 years relevant experience * Chartered status or equivalent qualification. * Knowledge of local legislation and its application a benefit. **What We Offer:** We offer a competitive salary package along with a comprehensive range of benefits designed to support your professional growth and enhance your overall well\-being: * **Competitive Salary:** We offer a competitive salary package to attract and retain top talent in the industry. * **Flexible Working:** Enjoy flexible working hours and hybrid working, allowing you to achieve a better work\-life balance. * **Employer Pension Match Scheme:** We offer a pension match scheme to help you secure your financial future. * **Income Protection:** Receive support in safeguarding your income in the event of illness or injury. * **Life Assurance:** Gain peace of mind with life assurance coverage. * **Training and Development Programme:** Access a range of training and development opportunities to enhance your skills and advance your career. * **Family \& Well\-Being Support:** Benefit from our Enhanced Maternity \& Paternity Benefits and our confidential support services to address personal or professional challenges, included in our Employee Assistance Programme. * **Sports and Social:** Engage in our vibrant sports and social activities to foster a sense of community and well\-being among our team members. * **Increased Annual Leave:** 25 days’ annual leave plus statutory bank holidays, enjoy increased annual leave entitlements based on years of service. * **Recognition Awards:** Receive recognition for your contributions through our awards program, celebrating achievements and excellence. *We are an equal opportunity employer and value diversity at our company. We do not discriminate based on race, religion, colour, national origin, gender, sexual orientation, age, marital status, veteran status, or disability status.* *We will ensure that individuals with disabilities are provided reasonable accommodation to participate in the job application or interview process, to perform crucial job functions, and receive other benefits and privileges of employment. Please contact us to request accommodation.*

title: Associate - Civil Engineering
description: **Associate \- Civil Engineering** *Dublin City Centre \| Senior Leadership Role \| Strategic Impact* CS Consulting is seeking an experienced Associate \- Civil Engineering to join our Dublin\-based leadership team. You’ll combine technical authority with commercial insight, managing projects from concept through construction and handover, while mentoring and developing engineers within the practice. challenging projects, including: * Large\-scale residential developments for the Land Development Agency (LDA) and Development Partners * High\-rise and low\-rise private residential projects nationwide * Refurbishment of historic structures for the OPW and other public/private clients * New\-build commercial offices and hotels * Fit\-out and refurbishment of commercial offices and hotels * Industrial and data centre projects **Key Responsibilities** * Lead the design and delivery of all civil infrastructure on significant projects * Provide technical and commercial leadership across project lifecycles, from early concept through construction * Collaborate closely with Directors, clients, and multidisciplinary design teams * Mentor, coach, and develop engineers within the practice * Ensure compliance with Irish standards, including Uisce Éireann publications, the Greater Dublin Regional Code of Practice for Drainage Works, and Local Authority guidelines * Manage interactions with Local Authorities, Statutory Bodies, and Utility Providers on behalf of clients * Oversee site delivery including BCAR inspections, submittals, RFI responses, snagging, and project closeout * Exercise independent professional judgement on complex technical and commercial matters * Manage project budgets, schedules, and client relationships **Candidate Profile** * Masters or Honours degree in Civil Engineering (or equivalent) * Chartered Engineer (Engineers Ireland and/or ICE) * Minimum 10 years’ post\-qualification experience in a consulting engineering environment * Proven leadership in delivering civil infrastructure across a broad range of project types * Strong conceptual, detailed design, and construction stage expertise * Excellent knowledge of the Irish planning system, typical planning reports, and statutory engagement * Proficiency in civil design software (InfoDrainage, Civil 3D, AutoCAD) and experience in coordinated BIM environments * High competence with Microsoft Office and project planning tools * Strong commercial awareness and experience managing project finances and client relationships * Excellent leadership, people management, and stakeholder engagement skills * Solutions\-focused, proactive, and adaptable mindset * Full Drivers Licence (desirable) **What We Offer** * Competitive base salary and defined bonus scheme * Generous annual leave allowance \+ company bonus day * Employer pension contribution * Opportunity to influence business strategy and direction * Exposure to high\-profile, technically diverse projects * Collaborative, forward\-thinking environment that invests in people, technology, and innovation * Genuine career progression opportunities and leadership development At CS Consulting, we value technical excellence, integrity, and collaboration. This is a chance to play a key role in shaping projects, teams, and the future of our business.

title: Associate Investment Management

title: Associate Legal Counsel - Privacy and Consumer Protection
description: **About Us** Temu is an e\-commerce platform that connects consumers with millions of merchandise partners, manufacturers and brands with the mission of empowering them to live better lives. Temu is committed to offering affordable products to enable consumers and merchandise partners to fulfill their dreams in an inclusive environment. Temu was founded in Boston, Massachusetts in 2022\. The headquarters for our European operations is located in Dublin. Today, we have expanded our footprint to more than 90 countries. **About the Role** Temu is seeking a highly skilled, privacy and consumer protection\-focused Associate Legal Counsel to join our Dublin\-based European legal team. In this role, you will contribute towards ensuring that our platform operates at the highest standards of data security, privacy and consumer protection compliance. In collaboration with your colleagues on the European legal team, you will focus on maintaining our data privacy and security, and consumer protection compliance programmes, and will carry out important research into upcoming regulatory requirements to ensure that we continue to safeguard consumer and regulator trust, while also supporting our ongoing operations and global expansion. This role is based in our Dublin, Ireland office. **Main Responsibilities** * **Strategic Compliance Frameworks** : Support in the management and maintenance of EU data privacy and security, and consumer protection compliance programmes, to ensure alignment with national and EU\-wide regulatory expectations. * **Risk Mitigation \& Audits:** Support internal and external compliance assessments; work closely with cross\-functional teams to proactively identify, track, and remediate data security, privacy, and other regulatory compliance risks. * **Data Governance** : Contribute to the development and maintenance of policies regarding data subject rights under the EU General Data Protection Regulation, the Digital Services Act and other applicable legislation, draft and maintain privacy compliance documents including data protection impact assessments, records of processing activities, legitimate interests assessments, data processing agreements, transfer impact assessments, data breach assessments and notifications, data subject access requests, to ensure our business remains in compliance with applicable regulatory requirements. * **Research** : Ensure that the business identifies early and can prepare for the impact of upcoming changes in legislation by proactively staying on top of developments in applicable law. * **Cross Disciplinary Assistance:** Provide occasional support to our Disputes and EU Competition Law functions, as the need arises, and contribute towards the development of cross\-company awareness of key litigation and EU Competition Law (including DMA) developments. * **Internal Advisory** : Partner with engineering, product, and operations teams to integrate high privacy standards into the development of all EU\-based products and services. * **Third\-Party Oversight** : Manage legal risks associated with vendors and partners, ensuring that data\-sharing ecosystems adhere to the company's rigorous security standards. **Qualifications and Abilities** * Education/Qualifications: Undergraduate degree or above and a qualified solicitor, barrister, or equivalent legal professional licensed to practise law in Ireland, the UK, and/or another EU Member State. * Experience: The ideal candidate will have 1\-3 years of post\-qualification experience, preferably specialising in privacy, data security, consumer protection or both. A background that includes both a premier law firm and an in\-house role in a highly regulated tech or consumer\-facing environment is favourable. * Regulatory Acumen: Experience navigating complex regulatory environments or implementing compliance programmes. * Technical Proficiency: Ability to translate complex legal requirements into actionable guidance for technical teams. * Execution Excellence: Exceptional project management skills, keen attention to detail and the ability to work independently in a dynamic environment with shifting priorities. * Fluency in English (both written and spoken) is a must. Proficiency in any other EU language(s) is a plus. **Compensation \& Benefits** The total salary for this position will be determined based on the candidate’s experience, qualifications, skills and competencies, ensuring a competitive and attractive offer. In addition, this position offers a comprehensive benefits package, including: * Medical and Dental Insurance Coverage * Pension The Company reserves the right to modify or change these benefit programs at any time, with or without notice, consistent with applicable law. At Temu, we are proud to be an employer that builds a diverse community. We encourage all qualified individuals to apply and join our team. We are dedicated to providing a work environment free from discrimination and harassment, where all employees are treated with respect and dignity. Apply today to join Temu and be part of a dynamic and innovative company.

title: Associate Microsoft Consultant | Data Security and Governance
description: **Associate Consultant – Microsoft Security** What you need to know before applying: \-\> Remote\-first, with occasional onsite work. The role is **based in Ireland or the UK.** *\-\>* No sponsorship offered. You need to be legally authorised to work full\-time in UK or Ireland. \-\> Interview process: video \+ onsite. Threatscape’s Microsoft Security Practice is growing, and we are looking for an **Associate Consultant** who is eager to learn, motivated to develop their technical skills, and confident communicating with both clients and colleagues. You’ll work closely with our experienced consultants, including five Microsoft MVPs, while building a strong foundation in Microsoft Security technologies. **This role is ideal for** someone with **early professional experience in IT or security (1–3 years)**, who has a good grasp of Microsoft technologies and a strong desire to grow into a worldclass security consultant. Above all, **we value people who have high agency, contagious enthusiasm, communicate clearly, enjoy collaborating, are curious, proactive learners, and show professionalism, empathy, and attention to detail.** *Ability to work independently in a remote\-first role is key.* If that sounds like you, we want to talk. **Key responsibilities** As an Associate Consultant, you will: * Support the delivery of Microsoft security, compliance, identity, and endpoint management solutions under guidance from senior consultants. For example: Entra, Defender, Purview, Intune, Azure, Copilot, and Active Directory. * Assist with customer engagements, including discovery sessions, documentation, demonstrations, and implementation. * Contribute to security reviews, basic assessments, and gap analyses. * Help prepare materials such as presentations, reports, and technical notes. * Learn to evaluate and advise on Microsoft security tools, gradually taking on more client\-facing work as you develop. * Collaborate with your colleagues on internal projects, tooling, service improvements, and knowledge sharing. You will not be expected to lead engagements immediately, but we will coach you toward that during the introductory period. **What you need to succeed** * 1–3 years’ experience in a technical or IT role (helpdesk, support, systems admin, cloud, networking, or security). * A solid understanding, or hands on familiarity, with some Microsoft technologies * Strong written and verbal communication skills. * Confidence working directly with people. * A genuine interest in cybersecurity, a willingness to continuously learn, ambitions to contribute to the cybersecurity community and our company’s growth, and an incredible degree of agency and enthusiasm. * Great organisational skills and the ability to manage tasks across multiple projects. **Qualifications (preferred but not mandatory)** Certifications would be a bonus, including: * SC900 * AZ900, SC300, or SC200 * Or any progress toward Microsoft cybersecurity certifications If you haven’t achieved these yet, we’ll support you in earning them. **Why Join Threatscape?** * Work with and learn from some of the top Microsoft Security experts, including 5 MVPs. * Grow your skills with real project experience and structured mentorship. Be supported in your learning path, with clear career progression into midlevel and senior consulting roles. * Join a Microsoft Solutions Partner for Security \& Modern Work with multiple Security Partner of the Year awards. * Work on meaningful security projects that make a real difference to our clients. Pay: €30,000\.00\-€45,000\.00 per year Benefits: * Bike to work scheme * Company events * Employee assistance program * Private dental insurance * Private medical insurance * Work from home Work Location: Hybrid remote in Sandyford, County Dublin

title: Associate People Operations Specialist
description: **Who We Are** At CarGurus (NASDAQ: CARG), our mission is to give people the power to reach their destination. We started as a small team of developers determined to bring trust and transparency to car shopping. Since then, our history of innovation and go\-to\-market acceleration has driven industry\-leading growth. In fact, we’re the largest and fastest\-growing automotive marketplace, and we’ve been profitable for over 15 years. **What We Do** The market is evolving, and we are too, moving the entire automotive journey online and guiding our customers through every step. That includes everything from the sale of an old car to the financing, purchase, and delivery of a new one. Today, tens of millions of consumers visit CarGurus.com each month, and \~30,000 dealerships use our products. But they're not the only ones who love CarGurus—our employees do, too. We have a people\-first culture that fosters kindness, collaboration, and innovation, and empowers our Gurus with tools to fuel their career growth. Disrupting a trillion\-dollar industry requires fresh and diverse perspectives. Come join us for the ride! **Role Overview** The Associate People Operations Specialist provides administrative and operational support across benefits, payroll, HR systems, and employee lifecycle processes. This role is critical to delivering accurate, compliant, and high\-quality People Operations support while enhancing the overall employee experience. The role focuses on executing established workflows, maintaining employee and benefits data, managing vendor coordination, and responding to employee queries. The ideal candidate is detail\-oriented, technically proficient, and capable of managing multiple priorities across global programs. **What You'll Do** **Benefits Administration \& Vendor Management** * Manage global benefits operations across the US, Canada, UK, and Ireland, overseeing benefit administration, invoice tracking, and the processing and reconciliation of payments across approximately 20 vendors (including medical, dental, and life insurance) * Conduct monthly audits of benefit plans, identifying discrepancies and driving resolution * Administer global pension and retirement schemes (UK, Ireland, US), including system uploads and quarterly fee tracking * Oversee FSA/HSA funding aligned with payroll cycles, including quarterly employer contributions * Administer COBRA processes and maintain associated tracking documentation * Manage Evidence of Insurability (EOI) approvals and send follow\-ups for pending submissions **HR Systems \& Data Management** * Maintain accurate employee data across Workday and related systems * Review and approve Qualified Life Event (QLE) benefit changes, including manual FSA/HSA adjustments * Process benefits onboarding, employee changes, beneficiary updates, and one\-time employee payments (e.g., bonuses, reimbursements). * Monitor and troubleshoot weekly benefits integration files to ensure data accuracy and integrity * Run and review reports including Terminated with Benefits, Business Process Transactions, and quarterly dependent/beneficiary audits **Employee Support \& Communications** * Manage employee queries via ticketing systems (e.g., JIRA) * Provide support for benefits platforms and respond to FAQs * Coordinate employee communications, including onboarding reminders, benefits education, and wellness program updates * Maintain global Benefits Wiki and internal documentation, ensuring accuracy and accessibility **Programs \& Operational Support** * Administer commuter and parking programs, including enrollments, terminations, and funding file coordination * Plan and coordinate on\-site and virtual events, including the annual Open Enrollment Fair * Support absence tracking, time\-off administration, and other operational tasks that enhance employee experience and team efficiency **Payroll \& Compliance** * Manage employee payroll deductions for Ireland and UK benefits * Support unemployment claim documentation and responses * Ensure compliance with regulatory requirements across benefits, payroll, and related programs **What You'll Bring** * 1–2 years of experience in benefits administration or HR operations * Strong proficiency in Google Sheets (or Microsoft Excel) to organize and analyze data, with a solid grasp of Pivot Tables and functions like VLOOKUP. Experience with HRIS systems, particularly Workday Benefits Administration module * Comfort working with data and systems * Strong data entry and reconciliation capabilities * Familiarity with benefits administration platforms across US / UK / IE * Understanding of system integrations and troubleshooting * Working knowledge of benefits regulations across all locations (US/ UK/ IE), including COBRA, FSA/HSA administration, pension contributions and associated tax implications (i.e UK/IE pension auto\-enrolment and payroll deductions for medical/dental benefits) * Basic understanding of life insurance and Evidence of Insurability processes * Familiarity with payroll processing and vendor management The displayed range represents the expected annual base salary / On\-Target Earnings (OTE) for this position. On\-Target Earnings (OTE) is inclusive of base salary and on\-target commission earnings, which applies exclusively to sales roles. Individual pay within this range is determined by work location and other factors such as job\-related skills, experience, and relevant education or training. This annual base salary forms part of a comprehensive Total Rewards Package. In addition to benefits, this role may qualify for discretionary bonuses/incentives and Restricted Stock Units (RSUs). Position Pay Range €37\.000—€46\.000 EUR **Working at CarGurus** We reward our Gurus’ curiosity and passion with best\-in\-class benefits and compensation, including equity for all employees, both when they start and as they continue to grow with us. Our career development and corporate giving programs, as well as our employee resource groups (ERGs) and communities, help people build connections while making an impact in personally meaningful ways. A flexible hybrid model and robust time off policies encourage work\-life balance and individual well\-being. Thoughtful perks like daily free lunch, a new car discount, meditation and fitness apps, commuting cost coverage, and more help our people create space for what matters most in their personal and professional lives. **We welcome all** CarGurus strives to be a place to which people can bring the ultimate expression of themselves and their potential—starting with our hiring process. We do not discriminate based on race, color, religion, national origin, age, sex, marital status, ancestry, physical or mental disability, veteran status, gender identity, or sexual orientation. We foster an inclusive environment that values people for their skills, experiences, and unique perspectives. That’s why we hope you’ll apply even if you don’t check every box listed in the job description. We also encourage you to tell your recruiter if you require accommodations to participate in our hiring process due to a disability so we can provide the appropriate support. We want to know what only you can bring to CarGurus.

title: Associate Professor in Public International Law
description: Closing Date : 11\-May\-2026 12:00 Contact Email : recruit@tcd.ie Trinity is Ireland’s leading university and is ranked 75th in the world (QS World University Rankings 2026\). Founded in 1592, the University is steeped in history with a reputation for excellence in education, research, and innovation, which has been inspiring generations of thinkers for over 400 years. Thrive: Trinity College Dublin's Strategic Plan 2025 \- 2030 **Post Summary:** Applications are welcome from legal scholars with internationally recognised research excellence in the field of public international law. The postholder will be required to deliver core modules at undergraduate level in the area of public international law, to develop and deliver postgraduate modules, and to supervise students at undergraduate, masters and doctoral levels. The successful candidate will be required to carry out administrative duties as directed by the Head of School and/or Head of Discipline and is expected to be research active. A key feature of this role is to provide leadership for the School’s LL.M. in International and Comparative Law programme and to drive the integration of public international law perspectives across the undergraduate curriculum. Interviews and presentations for this competition will take place online the week beginning 22 June 2026\. **Post status: Permanent Contract** **Hours of Post:** Hours of work for academic staff are those as prescribed under Public Service Agreements. title: Associate SIM Engineer
description: **The Company** Cubic³ provides advanced software\-defined vehicle solutions to over 200 countries around the world. Our powerfully smart connectivity enables leading automotive, agriculture, and transportation OEMs to deliver innovative new services and fully compliant in\-vehicle experiences that customers desire, regardless of local market requirements. We believe in leadership that supports empowerment and responsibility, while recognising and developing leadership qualities across Our Team. Together we bring out the best in each other. So, whether you’re interested in joining us as an individual contributor, manager, senior leader – or someone who aspires to growing into a leadership role – we look for people who are results focused, empathetic, visionary, empowering, and who ‘champion’ our cultures and values. **The Role** We are seeking an Associate SIM Engineer who is eager to learn and develop their expertise in SIM and eSIM technologies. You will join a dynamic SIM Engineering team, contributing to the delivery of Cubic solutions in the SIM/eSIM Remote SIM Provisioning technology domain. The Associate SIM Engineer will assist with building connectivity solutions, managing SIM/eSIM card logistics and fulfilment, and supporting our global customer base. You will have the opportunity to gain experience with the latest technologies, including the evolution of eSIM and new connectivity standards. **Responsibilities** * Assist in the design, validation and maintenance of electrical profiles for SIM and eSIM products * Support the design and implementation of Java Card applets for SIM/eSIM services, following 3GPP, ETSI, and GSMA standards * Help write and update technical documentation, such as applet specifications and test plans * Collaborate with cross\-functional teams across mobile core networks, security, and device vendors * Support the development, testing, and integration of SIM\-based applications and tools * Learn to use smart card test \& trace tools * Participate in peer reviews and team meetings **What’s In It For You** * Exposure to end\-to\-end SIM/eSIM ordering and provisioning * Opportunity to work with cutting edge IoT and connectivity technologies * Learning and development opportunities in a collaborative environment * Chance to contribute ideas and develop technical and organisational skills within a small, experienced team **Required Skills \& Experience** * Third level qualification in engineering, computer science, or a related discipline (or final year student/recent graduate) * Up to 2 years of relevant experience, preferably within telecoms technology or similar sectors * Familiarity with SIM/eSIM concepts and protocols (APDU, ISO 7816\) is a plus * Awareness of 3GPP, ETSI, or GSMA standards * Basic understanding of Java, Java Card, or scripting languages * Willingness to learn about mobile network elements and smart card technologies * Quality\-focused, self\-motivated, with analytical and problem\-solving skills * Effective written and verbal communication **Desirable** * Experience using tools like Git, Postman, or smart card test tools (e.g., Comprion) is a bonus * Interest in Kanban/Agile methodologies **Benefits** * 25 days annual leave \+ 2 company days * Health Insurance * Pension Contribution * ESOP * Tax saver scheme * Bike to work scheme * Sports and social activities * Annual office outings * Bits \& Bobs days (quarterly half days for personal tasks) * Learning and Development Opportunities Cubic³ is an equal opportunities employer and committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace. *New hires at* Cubic³ *are required to work onsite five days a week during their six\-month probation period to get to know the company, their team, and our ways of working. After probation, we offer a flexible arrangement of up to eight work\-from\-home days per month, provided it aligns with business needs and performance standards. This arrangement is not a given for all roles.* **Cubic³ is an equal opportunities employer and committed to fostering a diverse and inclusive workplace.**

title: Associate, Client Operations II
description: At BNY, our culture allows us to run our company better and enables employees’ growth and success. As a leading global financial services company at the heart of the global financial system, we influence nearly 20% of the world’s investible assets. Every day, our teams harness cutting\-edge AI and breakthrough technologies to collaborate with clients, driving transformative solutions that redefine industries and uplift communities worldwide. Recognized as a top destination for innovators, BNY is where bold ideas meet advanced technology and exceptional talent. Together, we power the future of finance – and this is what \#LifeAtBNY is all about. Join us and be part of something extraordinary. We’re seeking a future team member for the role of Associate, Client Operations II to join our Client Service team. This role is located in Dublin. * Day to day oversight of the assigned Collateralised Loan Obligations (“CLOs”) and /or loan portfolios to ensure high Client Experience Levels * Transaction Management: \- Cash \& Trade (Loans, Bonds etc) Management Oversight – ability to engage with Clients to ensure timely execution, Report Review and preparation on CLO and or loan Portfolio’s Performance, Monitoring of Service Level * Collaboration: Continuous dialogue and open communication with Lead Client Service Manager (CSM) and Group Manager on status of work being completed by supporting teams * Participate in Client Meetings and follow up items are closed out * Develop working relationships with Front Office Relationship Managers * Identify and resolve issues while serving as a high\-level escalation point for complex situations and ensure that they are brought to a successful resolution * Employee Development: Key role in training of support team on Client and deal specific requirements * Partner with Lead CSM to identify and streamline business operations. Support the efforts to enhance controls by standardising relevant business processes and consistently managing/monitoring those activities to ensure quality and accuracy. Support key business programs, including business and process improvements which drive greater operational efficiencies and superior client service * Support Lead CSM as an escalation point and communicate effectively with other internal shared services, external investment managers, brokers, and clients. This role sets the directions and tones for the client service administration elements of our business and therefore drives overall client relationships and risk management * Partner Management: proactively monitor and manage the activities performed within our business partners and hold regular meetings and escalate any concerns To be successful in this role, we’re seeking the following: * Corporate Trust or related financial markets/securities industry experience. * Strong Client Management Experience * Ability to review deal governing documents * Experience in Loans (syndicated, bi\-lateral, real estate) and CLOs very desirable * Solid understanding of the product(s) administered by the group and thorough understanding of risk management and control practices. * Proactively monitor and manage the activities performed within our business partners outside of Ireland * Ability to quickly address issues – strong decision making * Financial Services Technical Skills * Effective interpersonal, oral and written communication skills * Excellent analytical, organisational and time management skills with the ability to meet tight deadlines * Proficiency in using Microsoft packages particularly Excel At BNY, our culture speaks for itself, check out the latest BNY news at: BNY Newsroom BNY LinkedIn Here’s a few of our recent awards: * America’s Most Innovative Companies, Fortune, 2025 * World’s Most Admired Companies, Fortune 2025 * “Most Just Companies”, Just Capital and CNBC, 2025 Our Benefits and Rewards: BNY offers highly competitive compensation, benefits, and wellbeing programs rooted in a strong culture of excellence and our pay\-for\-performance philosophy. We provide access to flexible global resources and tools for your life’s journey. Focus on your health, foster your personal resilience, and reach your financial goals as a valued member of our team, along with generous paid leaves, including paid volunteer time, that can support you and your family through moments that matter. BNY is an Equal Employment Opportunity/Affirmative Action Employer \- Underrepresented racial and ethnic groups/Females/Individuals with Disabilities/Protected Veterans.

title: Associate, Guidelines Compliance (Dublin)
description: PIMCO is a global leader in active fixed income with deep expertise across public and private markets. We invest our clients’ capital across a range of fixed income and credit opportunities, leveraging our decades of experience navigating complex debt markets. Our flexible capital base and deep relationships with issuers have helped us become one of the world’s largest providers of traditional and nontraditional solutions for companies that need financing and investors who seek strong risk\-adjusted returns. Since 1971, our people have shaped our organization through a high\-performance inclusive culture, in which we celebrate diverse thinking. We invest in our people and strive to imprint our CORE values of Collaboration, Openness, Responsibility and Excellence. We believe each of us is here to help others succeed and this has led to PIMCO being recognized as an innovator, industry thought leader and trusted advisor to our clients. **Position Description** This is an exciting opportunity to work within the Legal \& Compliance department managing investment guidelines in PIMCO’s proprietary compliance system. The Guidelines Team is responsible for interpreting and implementing client, regulatory, and internal guidelines into the compliance system. The successful candidate will have broad exposure to the entire Investment Guideline Compliance process. Individuals in this position will have frequent interaction with a variety of groups at all levels of seniority including other teams within Legal \& Compliance, Account Management, Product Strategy and the Trade Floor. **On a Given Day, Your Work Might Include** * Collaborating with the Client Facing and Legal teams in negotiating investment discretions * Interpreting investment restrictions from different sources (including regulatory frameworks) * Supporting the Trade Floor with investment decisions * Directing the creation of rules within the compliance system * Researching and addressing various inquiries related to investment guidelines * Participating in numerous ad hoc projects tied to investment guideline interpretation **You Will Excel In This Role If You** * Have a strong sense of honesty and integrity * Quick learner and adaptable to learn new processes, concepts, and skills * Exhibit a strong commitment to quality and attention to detail * Possess excellent communication and interpersonal skills * Able to handle multiple, competing priorities and projects **You are going to need this to carry out the job:** * Minimum of a Bachelor’s Degree (Accounting, Business, Finance or Economics preferred) * 2\+ years of professional experience in compliance or the investment management industry * Basic to Intermediate knowledge of the global equity and fixed income markets, including an understanding of financial products such as bonds, equities, and derivatives. * Intermediate/advanced working knowledge of MS Excel. **The following are other qualifications that are preferred but not required:** * Experience with negotiating, interpreting and implementing guidelines in a compliance system is ideal * Knowledge of UCITS or other common legal/regulatory practices is a plus * Experience with Bloomberg, SQL or Business Objects * CFA designation or active status in the CFA program * German language skills Equal Employment Opportunity and Affirmative Action Statement *PIMCO recruits and hires qualified candidates without regard to race, national origin, ancestry, religion (including religious dress and grooming practices), sex (including pregnancy, childbirth, breastfeeding, or related medical conditions), sexual orientation, gender (including gender identity and expression), age, military or veteran status, disability (physical or mental), any factor prohibited by law, and as such affirms in policy and practice to support and promote the concept of equal employment opportunity and affirmative action, in accordance with all applicable federal, state, provincial and municipal laws. The company also prohibits discrimination on other basis such as medical condition, or marital status under applicable laws.* Applicants with Disabilities *PIMCO is an Equal Employment Opportunity/Affirmative Action employer. We provide reasonable accommodation for qualified individuals with disabilities, including veterans, in job application procedures. title: Associate, Investor Delivery - Private Equity
description: MUFG Investor Services is a trusted partner to many of the world’s largest public and private funds, providing asset servicing and operational solutions built for alternatives. With over $1 trillion in client assets under administration, we offer fund administration, banking, payments, fund financing, foreign exchange overlay, corporate and regulatory services, custody, business consulting, and more. title: Business Analyst Store Technology (FTC)
description: **12 \- 24 Months Fixed Term Contracts** Because we strive to put people first. Culture, our way. **Technology at Primark** What’s a career at Primark all about? The positive impact you’re making, the experiences you’re having and the people you’re with. You’re our inspiration. Embrace what sets you apart, own your career and develop in ways you never expected. Leave your mark. And do it your way. Our technology team is actively shaping the next wave of advancements. Engaged with innovative initiatives, your expertise will propel our business into the future. Collaborating with a creative team of tech enthusiasts, you’ll contribute your unique skills to fuel our technological advancements. **What You’ll Do As Business Analyst Store Technology** In your role, you’ll collaborate with different people across a range of skillsets. Here’s a flavour of your day\-to\-day: * Advise Product Owners and Project/Programme Managers on Primark’s business analysis methodology, ensuring effective identification, prioritisation, and validation of business and user requirements. * Capture user needs, pain points, and process gaps through stakeholder interviews, workshops, and documentation reviews, analysing both as\-is and to\-be processes. * Collaborate with architects, developers, and product teams to translate business and technical needs into clear, actionable requirements, user stories, and acceptance criteria. * Maintain strong knowledge of systems and processes, manage requirement prioritisation (e.g., MoSCoW), and ensure full traceability throughout the delivery lifecycle. * Support QA and UAT by clarifying expected system behaviour, triaging defects, and ensuring solutions meet business objectives. * Contribute to discovery, business case development, training, and adoption work while continuously improving and championing Primark’s business analysis methodology. **What You’ll Get** People are at the heart of what we do here, so it’s essential we provide you with the right environment to perform at your very best. They’re the beating heart of our business and the reason we’ve grown from our first store in Dublin in 1969 to a £9bn\+ turnover business and over 80,000 colleagues and over 440 stores in 17 countries today. Our values run through everything we do. In essence, we're Caring and always strive to put people first. We're also Dynamic, bravely pushing the boundaries to stay ahead. And finally, we succeed Together.
